page: Amazon Web Services mac-m4.metal is a bare metal instance featuring the Apple silicon architecture. It is equipped with 10 physical cores and 10 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz on the arm64_mac architecture. The system allocates dedicated CPU resources with a single thread per core. It includes 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.4 GB per core.

For local storage, the instance is bundled with a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, providing high-speed local storage capacity directly on the bare metal hardware. The network configuration delivers a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a dedicated hardware profile tailored for macOS environments. The 1:2.4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions it near compute-optimized thresholds, though the absolute memory capacity is fixed at 24.0 GB. The dedicated physical core allocation ensures consistent execution without virtualization overhead, representing a specific hardware class tradeoff where resource scaling is constrained by the physical Apple Mac mini hardware limits.

This instance is designed for workloads requiring native macOS environments. Primary use cases include iOS and macOS application compilation, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, automated testing of Apple ecosystem software, and general-purpose macOS development tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services mac-m4.metal is a bare metal macOS instance powered by a 10-core Apple processor using the arm64_mac architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz, the instance provides 10 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core. It is configured with 24.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.4 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by an integrated 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. As a dedicated bare metal offering, it eliminates virtualization overhead but is constrained by the fixed physical limits of the underlying hardware. This instance is designed for macOS-specific workloads, including iOS and macOS application building, automated testing, and CI/CD pipelines within the Apple software development ecosystem.

tagline: Dedicated bare metal macOS infrastructure featuring Apple silicon, integrated NVMe storage, and high-speed networking for application development pipelines.
bullet_points: 10 dedicated physical cores on Apple arm64_mac architecture at 3.5 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory providing 2.4 GB per core
Bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Designed for macOS and iOS build, test, and CI/CD workloads
